Scenario.title: Communication Infrastructure Compromise via Phishing
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple A basic cyber attack targeting a communications provider using phishing to gain initial access, executing scripts to establish control, and exfiltrating sensitive data through automated methods.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Malicious Payload, Command
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Command Execution (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends deceptive emails mimicking legitimate communication services to trick employees into clicking malicious links. This exploits human error to gain initial access to the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker deploys a malicious script via the compromised endpoint, leveraging command-line interpreters to execute payloads that establish persistent control over the system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker uses automated commands to steal encrypted communication logs and customer data, transmitting it to external servers disguised as routine traffic patterns.

Scenario.summary: A basic phishing campaign initiates access, followed by script-based execution and automated data exfiltration targeting a communications infrastructure.
